---
# Source Card: Risk Assessment of Cognitive Impairment at 2 Years of Age in Infants Born Extremely Preterm Using the INTERGROWTH-21st Growth Standards

## Summary
This peer-reviewed multicenter prognostic study (US NICHD Neonatal Research Network, 5,393 infants born at 24-26 weeks gestation, 2008-2018) used classification and regression trees (CART) to derive INTERGROWTH-21st z-score cut points at 36 weeks postmenstrual age (PMA) that flag risk of mild/moderate cognitive impairment (Bayley BSID-III cognitive composite score <85) at 2 years corrected age. It establishes that length and head-circumference z-scores outperform weight-based definitions for risk detection, and that the specific cut points depend on which growth standard (INTERGROWTH-21st vs Fenton) is used. For a pediatric CDS module, the CART-derived cut points are study-proposed thresholds (implementation proposals), while the standard definitions and outcome definitions are source-supported facts.

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) Alternative growth-failure weight cut point (CART): weight z-score <= -1.79 at 36 wk PMA, specificity 82% — locator: Results/Discussion para 1 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:84c3c5a201ab13f59cc4cd76f93d04cbb31794a65c23c26056c56e18c66103eb]"
- (ev_002) Length z-score < -1 at 36 wk PMA had highest sensitivity (80%) — locator: Results/Discussion — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:99f4e3f6a39659edcaade500335e02eb190524111f71d0d17a73aed15b8d127f]"
- (ev_003) HC z-score decline > 2.43 (birth to 36 wk PMA) had highest specificity (86%) — locator: Results/Discussion — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:450c3a61549bfeca6c6f77013d7092e8b665bcef4ffd3c5a7ee3b2b5f16a6d1c]"
- (ev_004) Alternative growth-faltering weight-decline cut point (CART): <= -1.06 birth to 36 wk PMA — locator: Results — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:38f34d73589c1341a41464b2dc38ad571a8b79dfa67446ba938b97e600d965dd]"
- (ev_005) Standard growth failure: weight z-score < -1.28 (=10th pct) at 36 wk PMA, 32% prevalence — locator: Results (baseline) — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:820e4a25c28d40f8311f856266ea70b238c90c5e057f5ba0cef2615a7df6ac25]"
- (ev_006) Standard growth faltering: weight z-score decline > 1.2 (birth to 36 wk PMA), sensitivity 34% — locator: Methods/Results — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:1aac435ebfdf378b372665eac1c945073908c29369d26771d6d09403dca834f0]"
- (ev_007) Cognitive-impairment outcome: BSID-III CCS < 85 (mild, 35%), < 70 (moderate, 13%) at 2 yr CGA — locator: Methods/Results — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:a542b1596613dfb3442c1dbbff2f26806419d501341960d10e2024233391f627]"
- (ev_008) Growth-standard dependence: Fenton curves give 57% growth failure vs 32% by INTERGROWTH-21st (same -1.28 cut point, same infants) — locator: Results — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:26ee53102df37c5bffcf26920ee49ac32329488876f6f788216ba918d5efb9b8]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population is narrowly bounded: infants born at 24-26 weeks gestation only (explicitly excludes 22-23 and 27 weeks), both sexes with sex-specific z-scores, evaluated at 36 weeks PMA (growth) and 2 years corrected gestational age (cognition).
- All thresholds are z-score / z-score-decline cut points (dimensionless; UCUM "1"), not raw anthropometry. They are method-dependent: computed with the INTERGROWTH-21st "Newborn Size for Very Preterm Infants" app (birth) and "Postnatal Growth of Preterm Infants" app (36 wk PMA). Substituting Fenton curves materially changes prevalence (ev_008).
- Lifecycle: CART-derived cut points are novel to this 2024 publication and are proposed, not ratified as a clinical standard; the -1.28 / >1.2 definitions are pre-existing standard definitions applied here.
- Note: the abstract rounds the CART cut points (weight failure -1.8; weight-faltering decline 1.1); the body reports -1.79 and -1.06. This card records the precise body values and the abstract rounding under `supersedes`.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- The paper frames a two-step risk-assessment tool (length z-score screen -> head-circumference-decline confirmation) intended to prompt "earlier individualized interventions" and referral to early-intervention programs for highest-risk infants. It is a risk-stratification/monitoring proposal, not a validated referral rule, and issues NO diagnosis/treatment/dosing directive.

## Limitations & conflicts
- CART cut points derived on a single cohort (development/testing split within the same NRN dataset); not externally validated as a deployable clinical rule — treat ev_001/ev_002/ev_003/ev_004 as implementation proposals for the CDS converter, never executable rules.
- CONFLICT (growth standard): Fenton growth curves yield 57% growth-failure prevalence vs 32% by INTERGROWTH-21st for the identical -1.28 cut point; Fenton-based faltering showed no cognitive-impairment association. Preserved as a standard-dependence conflict (ev_008), not averaged.
- CONFLICT (literature): the authors' own prior pilot study and prior small INTERGROWTH-21st/Fenton studies found NO significant postnatal-growth-to-cognition association; this larger study finds fair-to-low accuracy even for its best cut points. Do not overstate predictive value.
- Sensitivity/specificity/PPV/NPV and the CART node parameters reference figures/tables ("as shown in") whose numeric bodies are not reproduced in the retrieved full text; only the values quoted verbatim above are asserted. Any additional per-table numbers are a GAP (table contents not locatable in retrieved text).
- Outcome-definition limit: "cognitive impairment" (BSID-III CCS <85) is not universally accepted and may exclude infants with severe global impairment or cerebral palsy; 2-year Bayley scores have known predictive limits.
- Not method-independent and not applicable outside 24-26 weeks gestation; missingness for 22-23 and 27-week infants is explicit (not treated as normal).
